title: GOV.UK Pay API Vocabulary
description: Terms and concepts used in the GOV.UK Pay API

operations.description: You can use this endpoint to search for recurring payments agreements. The agreements are sorted by date, with the most recently-created agreements appearing first.

operations.summary: Create an agreement for recurring payments
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to create a new agreement.

operations.summary: Get information about a single agreement for recurring payments
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to get information about a single recurring payments agreement.

operations.summary: Cancel an agreement for recurring payments
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to cancel a recurring payments agreement in the `active` status.

operations.summary: Send card details to authorise a MOTO payment
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to [authorise payments](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/moto_payments/moto_send_card_details_api/) you have created with `authorisation_mode` set to `moto_api`.
You can use this endpoint to search disputes. A dispute is when [a paying user challenges a completed payment through their bank](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/disputes/).

You can use this endpoint to [search for payments you’ve previously created](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/reporting/#search-payments/). Payments are sorted by date, with the most recently-created payment appearing first.
You can use this endpoint to [create a new payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/making_payments/).

operations.summary: Get information about a single payment
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to [get details about a single payment you’ve previously created](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/reporting/#get-information-about-a-single-payment).
You can use this endpoint [to cancel an unfinished payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/making_payments/#cancel-a-payment-that-s-in-progress).
You can use this endpoint to [take (‘capture’) a delayed payment from the paying user’s bank account](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/delayed_capture/).

You can use this endpoint to [get a list of a payment’s events](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/reporting/#get-a-payment-s-events). A payment event is when a payment’s `state` changes, such as when the payment is created, or when the paying user submits their details.

operations.operationId: Get all refunds for a payment
operations.summary: Get information about a payment’s refunds
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to [get a list of refunds for a payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/refunding_payments/#get-all-refunds-for-a-single-payment).

operations.operationId: Submit a refund for a payment
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to [fully or partially refund a payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/refunding_payments).

operations.summary: Check the status of a refund
operations.description: You can use this endpoint to [get details about an individual refund](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/refunding_payments/#checking-the-status-of-a-refund).

You can use this endpoint to [search refunds you’ve previously created](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/refunding_payments/#searching-refunds). The refunds are sorted by date, with the most recently created refunds appearing first.

properties.name: city
properties.description: The paying user's city.

properties.name: country
properties.description: The paying user’s country, displayed as a 2-character ISO-3166-1-alpha-2 code.

properties.name: line1
properties.description: The first line of the paying user’s address.

properties.name: line2
properties.description: The second line of the paying user’s address.

properties.name: postcode
properties.description: The paying user's postcode.

properties.name: agreement_id
properties.description: The unique ID GOV.UK Pay automatically associated with this agreement when you created it.

properties.name: cancelled_date
properties.description: The date and time this agreement was cancelled. This value uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) and ISO 8601 format – `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ`.

properties.name: created_date
properties.description: The date and time you created this agreement. This value uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) and ISO 8601 format – `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.sssZ`.

properties.name: description
properties.description: The description you sent when creating this agreement.

properties.name: reference
properties.description: The reference you sent when creating this agreement.

properties.name: status
properties.description: The status of this agreement. You can [read more about the meanings of each agreement status.](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/recurring_payments/#understanding-agreement-status)

properties.name: user_identifier
properties.description: The identifier you sent when creating this agreement. `user_identifier` helps you identify users in your records.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Number of agreements on the current page of search results.

properties.name: page
properties.description: The [page of agreements you’re viewing](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/api_reference/#pagination). To view other pages, make this request again using the `page` parameter.

properties.name: results
properties.description: Contains agreements matching your search criteria.

properties.name: total
properties.description: Total number of agreements matching your search criteria.

properties.name: card_number
properties.description: The full card number from the paying user's card.

properties.name: cardholder_name
properties.description: The name on the paying user's card.

properties.name: cvc
properties.description: The card verification code (CVC) or card verification value (CVV) on the paying user's card.

properties.name: expiry_date
properties.description: The expiry date of the paying user's card. This value must be in `MM/YY` format.

properties.name: one_time_token
properties.description: This single use token authorises your request and matches it to a payment. GOV.UK Pay generated the `one_time_token` when the payment was created.

properties.name: card_brand
properties.description: The brand of card the user paid with.

properties.name: card_type
properties.description: The type of card the user paid with.`null` means your user paid with Google Pay or we did not recognise which type of card they paid with.

properties.name: wallet_type
properties.description: The digital wallet type that the user paid with

properties.name: agreement_payment_type
properties.description: When a standing order agreement transaction is initiated we have to include an initiated reason attribute.This can have a value of `instalment`, `recurring`, or `unscheduled`.We must have a `set_up_agreement` property or you set `authorisation_mode` to `agreement` for the API to accept the AgreementPaymentType.

properties.name: amount
properties.description: Sets the amount the user will pay, in pence.

properties.name: authorisation_mode
properties.description: Sets how you intend to authorise the payment. Defaults to `web`. Payments created with `web` mode follow the [standard GOV.UK Pay payment journey](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/payment_flow/). Paying users visit the `next_url` in the response to complete their payment. Payments created with `agreement` mode are authorised with an agreement for recurring payments. If you create an `agreement` payment, you must also send an active `agreement_id`. You must not send `return_url`, `email`, or `prefilled_cardholder_details` or your request will fail. Payments created with `moto_api` mode return an `auth_url_post` object and a `one_time_token`. You can use `auth_url_post` and `one_time_token` to send the paying user’s card details through the API and complete the payment. If you create a `moto_api` payment, do not send a `return_url` in your request.

properties.name: delayed_capture
properties.description: You can use this parameter to [delay taking a payment from the paying user’s bank account](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/delayed_capture/#delay-taking-a-payment). For example, you might want to do your own anti-fraud checks on payments, or check that users are eligible for your service. Defaults to `false`.

properties.name: email
properties.description: email

properties.name: language
properties.description: [Sets the language of the user’s payment page](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/optional_features/welsh_language) with an ISO-6391 Alpha-2 code of a supported language.

properties.name: moto
properties.description: You can use this parameter to [designate a payment as a Mail Order / Telephone Order (MOTO) payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/moto_payments).

properties.name: return_url
properties.description: The URL [the paying user is directed to after their payment journey on GOV.UK Pay ends](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/making_payments/#choose-the-return-url-and-match-your-users-to-payments).

properties.name: set_up_agreement
properties.description: Use this parameter to set up an existing agreement for recurring payments. The `set_up_agreement` value you send must be a valid `agreement_id`.

properties.name: payment_id
properties.description: The unique ID GOV.UK Pay automatically associated with this payment when you created it.

properties.name: provider_id
properties.description: The reference number your payment service provider associated with the payment.

properties.name: dispute_id
properties.description: The unique ID GOV.UK Pay automatically associated with this dispute when the paying user disputed the payment.

properties.name: evidence_due_date
properties.description: The deadline for submitting your supporting evidence. This value uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) and ISO 8601 format

properties.name: fee
properties.description: The payment service provider’s dispute fee, in pence.

properties.name: net_amount
properties.description: The amount, in pence, your payment service provider will take for a lost dispute. 'net_amount' is deducted from your payout after you lose the dispute. For example, a 'net_amount' of '-1500' means your PSP will take £15.00 from your next payout into your bank account. 'net_amount' is always a negative value. 'net_amount' only appears if you lose the dispute.

properties.name: reason
properties.description: The reason the paying user gave for disputing this payment. Possible values are: 'credit_not_processed', 'duplicate', 'fraudulent', 'general', 'product_not_received', 'product_unacceptable', 'unrecognised', 'subscription_cancelled', >'other'

properties.name: code
properties.description: A GOV.UK Pay API error code. You can [find out more about this code in our documentation](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/api_reference/#gov-uk-pay-api-error-codes).

properties.name: requested
properties.description: Indicates whether an exemption was requested for the given payment.

properties.name: type
properties.description: Indicates the type of exemption. Only present for corporate exemption

properties.name: href
properties.description: A URL that lets you perform additional actions to this payment when combined with the associated `method`.

properties.name: method
properties.description: An API method that lets you perform additional actions to this paymentwhen combined with the associated `href`.

properties.name: result
properties.description: The outcome of the requested exemption

properties.name: corporate_card_surcharge
properties.description: The [corporate card surcharge](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/corporate_card_surcharges/#add-corporate-card-fees) amount in pence.

properties.name: total_amount
properties.description: Amount your user paid in pence, including corporate card fees. `total_amount` only appears if you [added a corporate card surcharge to the payment](https://docs.payments.service.gov.uk/corporate_card_surcharges/#add-corporate-card-fees).

properties.name: updated
properties.description: When this payment’s state changed. This value uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) and ISO-8601 format - `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.SSSZ`.

properties.name: refund_amount_available
properties.description: Amount in pence. Total amount still available before issuing the refund

properties.name: capture_submit_time
properties.description: The date and time GOV.UK Pay asked your payment service provider to take the payment from your user’s account. This value uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) and ISO 8601 format - `YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ss.SSSZ`

properties.name: captured_date
properties.description: The date your payment service provider took the payment from your user. This value uses ISO 8601 format - `YYYY-MM-DD`

properties.name: settled_date
properties.description: The date that the transaction was paid into the service's account.

properties.name: can_retry
properties.description: If `can_retry` is `true`, you can use this agreement to try to take another recurring payment. If `can_retry` is `false`, you cannot take another recurring payment with this agreement. `can_retry` only appears on failed payments that were attempted using an agreement for recurring payments.

properties.name: finished
properties.description: Indicates whether a payment journey is finished.

properties.name: message
properties.description: A description of what went wrong with this payment. `message` only appears if the payment failed.

properties.name: refund_id
properties.description: The unique ID GOV.UK Pay automatically associated with this refund when you created it.

properties.name: amount_available
properties.description: How much you can refund to the user, in pence.

properties.name: amount_submitted
properties.description: How much you’ve already refunded to the user, in pence.

properties.name: field
properties.description: The parameter in your request that's causing the error.

properties.name: header
properties.description: The header in your request that's causing the error.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Indicates if this payment was authorised with 3D Secure authentication. `required` is `true` if the payment required 3D Secure authentication.
